title: mixpower Handoff
text: Purpose: Simulation-based power sample-size analysis linear generalized linear mixed-effects models (LMMs GLMMs). Design-first (pilot data required); explicit seeds full diagnostics. Repository: https://github.com/alitovchenko/mixpowerVersion: 0.1.0 (tag v0.1.0 released).

title: mixpower Handoff
text: Design / assumptions: mp_design(clusters, trials_per_cell), mp_assumptions(fixed_effects, residual_sd, icc). Scenarios: mp_scenario(formula, design, assumptions, test, simulate_fun, fit_fun, test_fun) custom engines; convenience constructors . Power: mp_power(scenario, nsim, alpha, seed, failure_policy, conf_level, keep) — single design, returns power + MCSE + CI + diagnostics (failure_rate, singular_rate). Sensitivity: mp_sensitivity(scenario, vary, nsim, ...) — grid one parameters; returns results table estimate, conf_low/high, failure_rate, singular_rate, n_effective per cell. Power curve: mp_power_curve(scenario, vary, ...) — one-parameter sensitivity; plot() (1D line 2D heatmap two vary params), summary(). Parallel curve: mp_power_curve_parallel(scenario, vary, workers, ..., progress) — curve per-cell seeds; parallel progress = FALSE, serial progress bar progress = TRUE. Sample-size solver: mp_solve_sample_size(scenario, parameter, grid, target_power, ...) — minimum grid value achieving target power. Helper: mp_grid_sample_size(, , length.= NULL, = NULL) build grid. Quick power (LMM): mp_quick_power(formula, clusters, trials_per_cell, fixed_effects, residual_sd, nsim, ...) — composes design, assumptions, mp_scenario_lme4, mp_power; ... passed mp_power() .

title: mixpower Handoff
text: Gaussian LMM: mp_backend_lme4(), mp_scenario_lme4(). Binomial GLMM: mp_scenario_lme4_binomial(), simulate_glmm_binomial_data(). Poisson GLMM: mp_scenario_lme4_poisson(), simulate_glmm_poisson_data(). Negative Binomial GLMM: mp_scenario_lme4_nb(), simulate_glmm_nb_data() (assumptions can include theta). lme4 scenarios support test_method = "wald" "lrt"; LRT requires explicit null_formula.

title: mixpower Handoff
text: Manifest: mp_manifest(scenario, seed, session) — scenario digest (formula/design/assumptions/test ), seed strategy, R/mixpower versions, timestamp, optional git SHA sessionInfo. Bundle: mp_bundle_results(result, manifest, study_id, analyst, notes) — result + manifest + labels; result mp_power, mp_sensitivity, mp_power_curve. Report table: mp_report_table(x) — flat data frame (power_estimate, ci_low/high, failure_rate, singular_rate, n_effective, nsim); works power/sensitivity/curve bundle. Export: mp_write_results(x, file, format = "csv" | "json", ...) — CSV = report table; JSON = report + manifest + labels bundles. JSON requires jsonlite (Suggests).

title: mixpower Handoff
text: Internal helpers: .assert_*, .stop, %||% (export). Failures never silently suppressed; diagnostics (failure_rate, singular_rate, n_effective) always exposed. mp_power() backend-agnostic; inference/backend logic lives scenario constructors backend helpers.

title: Effect sizes: maximal models, fitted pilots, and safeguard power
text: within-participant manipulation usually varies within every subject, “maximal” random-effects structure includes random slope . Ignoring real random slope inflates Type error rate, simulating — fitting — maximal model gives honest power. random_effects takes per-group intercept_sd, optional named list slopes, optional correlation cor (scalar applied every pair terms, full correlation matrix). fixed effect name also becomes balanced design predictor, several effects crossed factorial. single within-subject factor machinery reduces familiar (1 + condition | subject) form one slope intercept-slope correlation.
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 20), trials_per_cell = 6)  a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, x1 = 0.5, x2 = 0.3),   random_effects = list(subject = list(     intercept_sd = 0.4,     slopes = list(x1 = 0.3, x2 = 0.3),     cor = 0.1   )),   residual_sd = 1 ) a #> <mp_assumptions> #>   fixed_effects: #>     - (Intercept): 0 #>     - x1: 0.5 #>     - x2: 0.3 #>   random_effects (SD on linear predictor): #>     - subject: intercept_sd = 0.4, slope_sd(x1 = 0.3, x2 = 0.3), cor = 0.1  #>   residual_sd: 1 scn_max <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ x1 + x2 + (1 + x1 + x2 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a,   predictor = "x1" )  mp_power(scn_max, nsim = 15, seed = 2024)$power #> Warning: This design has few clusters and/or a complex random-effects #> structure, where Type I error is easy to get wrong. Check it with #> mp_calibrate() (or run mp_plan(), which calibrates and powers together) and see #> mp_recommend_method() for the inference method. A power estimate is only #> trustworthy if the test holds its alpha. #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> [1] 0.7333333

what: Power from a fitted pilot model
title: Effect sizes: maximal models, fitted pilots, and safeguard power
text: pilot published model, mp_from_fit() turns scenario: new responses simulated fit (keeping estimated variance components), model refit, focal term tested. fixed effects start fitted coefficients can varied planning.
code: m <- lme4::lmer(Reaction ~ Days + (Days | Subject), data = lme4::sleepstudy) scn_fit <- mp_from_fit(m, test_term = "Days") scn_fit$assumptions #> <mp_assumptions> #>   fixed_effects: #>     - (Intercept): 251.405 #>     - Days: 10.4673 #>   random_effects (SD on linear predictor): #>     - Subject: intercept_sd = 24.7407, slope_sd(Days = 5.92214), cor = 0.0655512  #>   residual_sd: 25.5918 mp_power(scn_fit, nsim = 15, seed = 1)$power #> [1] 1

what: Planning around a smallest effect size of interest
title: Effect sizes: maximal models, fitted pilots, and safeguard power
text: Pilot published effects often optimistic. robust habit plan power smallest effect still care . mp_sesoi() returns copy scenario focal effect replaced — either explicitly, scaling assumed effect (default multiplier = 0.85 conservative 15% reduction). Sweeping multiplier shows quickly power erodes effect interest shrinks — whole point planning SESOI rather (large) pilot estimate:
code: scn_sesoi <- mp_sesoi(scn_fit, multiplier = 0.85) c(   full  = scn_fit$assumptions$fixed_effects$Days,   sesoi = scn_sesoi$assumptions$fixed_effects$Days ) #>      full     sesoi  #> 10.467286  8.897193 mults <- c(1, 0.5, 0.3, 0.2) powers <- vapply(   mults,   function(mult) mp_power(mp_sesoi(scn_fit, multiplier = mult), nsim = 15, seed = 1)$power,   numeric(1) ) data.frame(multiplier = mults, effect = mults * scn_fit$assumptions$fixed_effects$Days, power = powers) #>   multiplier    effect     power #> 1        1.0 10.467286 1.0000000 #> 2        0.5  5.233643 0.8000000 #> 3        0.3  3.140186 0.4666667 #> 4        0.2  2.093457 0.3333333

title: Effect sizes: maximal models, fitted pilots, and safeguard power
text: safeguard effect (Perugini, Gallucci & Costantini, 2014) goes one step : instead point estimate, uses confidence-interval bound nearest zero, sampling uncertainty pilot propagated plan. mp_safeguard_effect() computes ; feed result straight mp_sesoi(). safeguard effect (lower confidence bound) smaller point estimate. sleepstudy Days effect large enough even conservative bound stays well powered; borderline effect, buffer exactly stops -optimistic pilot promising power deliver — compare multiplier sweep .
code: sg <- mp_safeguard_effect(m, term = "Days", conf_level = 0.90) sg #> <mp_safeguard> #>   term:        Days #>   estimate:    10.4673 (se 1.54579) #>   90% CI:     [7.92469, 13.0099] #>   safeguard:   7.92469 scn_safe <- mp_sesoi(scn_fit, effect = sg) mp_power(scn_safe, nsim = 15, seed = 1)$power #> [1] 1

what: Reading Type S and Type M errors
title: Effect sizes: maximal models, fitted pilots, and safeguard power
text: Every mp_power() run reports two diagnostics go beyond power figure (Gelman & Carlin, 2014): Type S (sign): among significant replicates, fraction wrong sign. Type M (magnitude): among significant replicates, average ratio estimated true effect — exaggeration factor. low power, significant estimates systematically inflated (Type M well 1); high power well calibrated. Type M near 1 means significant estimates right; value like 1.5 means published “hits” design overstate effect ~50%. planning SESOI safeguard effect — powering well — matters replicable findings.
code: under <- mp_power(mp_sesoi(scn_fit, multiplier = 0.25), nsim = 25, seed = 7) summary(under) #> $power #> [1] 0.44 #>  #> $mcse #> [1] 0.09927739 #>  #> $ci #> [1] 0.2440237 0.6507184 #>  #> $ci_method #> [1] "clopper-pearson" #>  #> $diagnostics #> $diagnostics$fail_rate #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$singular_rate #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_s #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_m #> [1] 1.595777 #>  #>  #> $nsim #> [1] 25 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $failure_policy #> [1] "count_as_nondetect" #>  #> $conf_level #> [1] 0.95 under$diagnostics$type_m #> [1] 1.595777

title: Extending mixpower with custom backends
text: mp_scenario stores engine : simulate_fun(scenario, seed = NULL) — returns data.frame simulated data.mp_power() passes seed function accepts (see ?mp_power). fit_fun(data, scenario) — returns fitted model object. test_fun(fit, scenario) — returns list(p_value = <scalar>). Use NA_real_ p_value test computed. mp_power() records fit_ok, optional singular attr(fit, "singular"), aggregates power using alpha failure_policy.

what: Building a validated backend with mp_backend()
title: Extending mixpower with custom backends
text: Use mp_backend() wrap three functions name optional metadata.validate_mp_backend() checks formal argument names run simulations:
code: library(mixpower)  sim_fun <- function(scenario, seed = NULL) {   n <- scenario$design$clusters$subject   x <- stats::rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)   y <- scenario$assumptions$fixed_effects$condition * x +     stats::rnorm(n, sd = scenario$assumptions$residual_sd)   data.frame(y = y, condition = x) } fit_fun <- function(data, scenario) stats::lm(scenario$formula, data = data) test_fun <- function(fit, scenario) {   sm <- summary(fit)   p <- sm$coefficients["condition", "Pr(>|t|)"]   list(p_value = as.numeric(p)) }  eng <- mp_backend(sim_fun, fit_fun, test_fun, name = "toy_lm") eng #> <mp_backend> #>   name: toy_lm  #>   simulate_fun: list(scenario = ) ...

title: Extending mixpower with custom backends
code: d <- mp_design(list(subject = 25), trials_per_cell = 1) a <- mp_assumptions(list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.2), residual_sd = 1) scn <- mp_scenario(   y ~ condition, d, a,   test = "custom",   simulate_fun = eng$simulate_fun,   fit_fun = eng$fit_fun,   test_fun = eng$test_fun ) mp_power(scn, nsim = 12, seed = 1) #> <mp_power> #>   nsim: 12 #>   alpha: 0.05 #>   failure_policy: count_as_nondetect #>   power: 0.000 #>   mcse: 0.000 #>   95% CI (clopper-pearson): [0.000, 0.265] #>   diagnostics: #>     - fail_rate: 0.000 #>     - singular_rate: 0.000 #>     - type_s: NA #>     - type_m: NA

title: mixpower intro
text: MixPower supports two inferential paths lme4 backend: "wald" (default): coefficient z-test fixed-effect estimate SE. "lrt": likelihood-ratio test explicit null full models. LRT, must provide null_formula explicitly. avoids hidden model-reduction rules keeps assumptions transparent.  Interpretation notes: Differences estimate reflect inferential method sensitivity DGP. Compare failure_rate singular_rate alongside power; higher failures can depress usable inference. Keep nsim modest examples, increase final study planning runs. Prefer explicit null_formula documentation reports LRT reproducibility.

title: mixpower intro
text: MixPower supports binomial GLMM power analysis via lme4::glmer() backend. keeps design assumptions explicit diagnostics visible. Extremely large effect sizes can lead quasi-separation. cases, glmer() may warn return unstable estimates. MixPower records failed simulations (via NA p-values), preserving transparency.
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 20), trials_per_cell = 6) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.5),   residual_sd = 1,   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.4)) )  scn_bin <- mp_scenario_lme4_binomial(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a,   test_method = "wald" )  res_bin <- mp_power(scn_bin, nsim = 12, seed = 123) #> Warning: This design has few clusters and/or a complex random-effects #> structure, where Type I error is easy to get wrong. Check it with #> mp_calibrate() (or run mp_plan(), which calibrates and powers together) and see #> mp_recommend_method() for the inference method. A power estimate is only #> trustworthy if the test holds its alpha. #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') summary(res_bin) #> $power #> [1] 0.08333333 #>  #> $mcse #> [1] 0.07978559 #>  #> $ci #> [1] 0.002107593 0.384796165 #>  #> $ci_method #> [1] "clopper-pearson" #>  #> $diagnostics #> $diagnostics$fail_rate #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$singular_rate #> [1] 0.25 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_s #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_m #> [1] 2.663587 #>  #>  #> $nsim #> [1] 12 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $failure_policy #> [1] "count_as_nondetect" #>  #> $conf_level #> [1] 0.95

title: mixpower intro
text: MixPower supports count outcomes Poisson Negative Binomial GLMMs. Poisson appropriate variance ≈ mean; NB handles -dispersion.
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 20), trials_per_cell = 6) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.4),   residual_sd = 1,   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.3)) )  scn_pois <- mp_scenario_lme4_poisson(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a,   test_method = "wald" )  res_pois <- mp_power(scn_pois, nsim = 12, seed = 123) #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') summary(res_pois) #> $power #> [1] 0.8333333 #>  #> $mcse #> [1] 0.1075829 #>  #> $ci #> [1] 0.5158623 0.9791375 #>  #> $ci_method #> [1] "clopper-pearson" #>  #> $diagnostics #> $diagnostics$fail_rate #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$singular_rate #> [1] 0.08333333 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_s #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_m #> [1] 1.18558 #>  #>  #> $nsim #> [1] 12 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $failure_policy #> [1] "count_as_nondetect" #>  #> $conf_level #> [1] 0.95  a_nb <- a a_nb$theta <- 1.5  # NB dispersion (size parameter)  scn_nb <- mp_scenario_lme4_nb(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a_nb,   test_method = "wald" )  res_nb <- mp_power(scn_nb, nsim = 12, seed = 123) #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #>  #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #>  #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') summary(res_nb) #> $power #> [1] 0.4166667 #>  #> $mcse #> [1] 0.1423188 #>  #> $ci #> [1] 0.1516522 0.7233303 #>  #> $ci_method #> [1] "clopper-pearson" #>  #> $diagnostics #> $diagnostics$fail_rate #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$singular_rate #> [1] 0.3333333 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_s #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_m #> [1] 1.574641 #>  #>  #> $nsim #> [1] 12 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $failure_policy #> [1] "count_as_nondetect" #>  #> $conf_level #> [1] 0.95

title: Reproducibility workflow
text: Run analysis usual create manifest scenario seed used. manifest records scenario digest (can verify design/assumptions later), seed strategy, R mixpower versions, optional session info, git SHA repo.
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 30), trials_per_cell = 4) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.3),   residual_sd = 1,   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.1)) ) scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a,   test_method = "wald" )  seed <- 123 res <- mp_power(scn, nsim = 12, seed = seed) #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') manifest <- mp_manifest(scn, seed = seed, session = FALSE) manifest #> <mp_manifest> #>   scenario_digest: fa4fbd6e1e4619ee ... #>   seed:123 (fixed) #>   timestamp: 2026-06-27 18:21:06 UTC  #>   r_version: 4.6.1  #>   mixpower_version: 1.2.0  #>   git_sha: afdfbd3

code: bundle <- mp_bundle_results(   res,   manifest,   study_id = "power_2024_01",   analyst = "analyst",   notes = "Initial power run for condition effect" ) bundle #> <mp_bundle> #>   result: mp_power  #>   study_id: power_2024_01  #>   analyst: analyst  #> <mp_manifest> #>   scenario_digest: fa4fbd6e1e4619ee ... #>   seed:123 (fixed) #>   timestamp: 2026-06-27 18:21:06 UTC  #>   r_version: 4.6.1  #>   mixpower_version: 1.2.0  #>   git_sha: afdfbd3 tab <- mp_report_table(bundle) tab #>   power_estimate     ci_low   ci_high failure_rate singular_rate type_s #> 1      0.1666667 0.02086253 0.4841377            0          0.25      0 #>     type_m n_effective nsim #> 1 1.713297          12   12 mp_write_results(bundle, "power_results.csv", format = "csv", row.names = FALSE) mp_write_results(bundle, "power_results.json", format = "json")

Use seed manifest: mp_power(scn, nsim = 20, seed = manifest$seed). Use nsim, alpha, failure_policy original run (store notes bundle labels needed). what: Calibrate: does the test hold its alpha?
title: Validation: is your power number trustworthy?
text: mp_calibrate() runs scenario null (focal effect set zero) estimates empirical Type error rate, exact interval verdict. correctly specified model sit near alpha.
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 24), trials_per_cell = 6) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.4),   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5)),   residual_sd = 1 ) scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ condition + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a)  mp_calibrate(scn, nsim = 60, seed = 11) #> <mp_calibration> #>   term:    condition (true effect set to 0) #>   nominal alpha: 0.05 #>   empirical Type I: 0.0500  (95% CI 0.0104, 0.1392) #>   verdict: well-calibrated

previous_headings: Calibrate: does the test hold its alpha?
title: Validation: is your power number trustworthy?
text: classic trap: data -subject random slope, analysis model omits . inflates Type error (Barr et al., 2013). mp_calibrate() flags . verdict turns anti-conservative: power computed model inflated. fix fit maximal model (1 + condition | subject), mixpower simulates tests directly.
code: a_slope <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.4),   random_effects = list(subject = list(     intercept_sd = 0.5, slopes = list(condition = 0.8)   )),   residual_sd = 1 ) # Data have the slope; the fitted model (1 | subject) ignores it. scn_mis <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ condition + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a_slope)  mp_calibrate(scn_mis, nsim = 60, seed = 7) #> <mp_calibration> #>   term:    condition (true effect set to 0) #>   nominal alpha: 0.05 #>   empirical Type I: 0.1667  (95% CI 0.0829, 0.2852) #>   verdict: anti-conservative #>   -> This test rejects too often under the null; its power is not #>      trustworthy. Try a df-corrected or bootstrap test, or a model #>      that matches the data-generating random-effects structure.

title: Validation: is your power number trustworthy?
text: Wald (z/t) tests anti-conservative number clusters small, degrees freedom overstated (Luke, 2017). mp_recommend_method() gives fast, design-based guidance. clusters steers toward degrees--freedom-corrected test (Satterthwaite Kenward-Roger LMMs) parametric bootstrap. can measure improvement mp_calibrate():
code: scn_few <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = mp_design(list(subject = 12), trials_per_cell = 8),   assumptions = a ) mp_recommend_method(scn_few) #> <mp_recommendation> #>   current method: wald #>   smallest grouping factor: 12 levels #>   caution: yes #>   recommended: kenward-roger, satterthwaite, pb #>   Smallest grouping factor has 12 levels (< 30). 'wald' tests can be anti-conservative with few clusters; prefer 'kenward-roger' or 'satterthwaite' or 'pb'. Verify with mp_calibrate(). scn_kr <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = mp_design(list(subject = 12), trials_per_cell = 8),   assumptions = a, test_method = "kenward-roger" ) mp_calibrate(scn_kr, nsim = 40, seed = 3)$type1 #> [1] 0.075

title: Validation: is your power number trustworthy?
text: Calibration results drop reporting layer power results: Run mp_calibrate() whenever change design size analysis model. trustworthy power analysis reports power calibration backs .
code: mp_report_table(mp_calibrate(scn, nsim = 40, seed = 1)) #>        term alpha type1     ci_low   ci_high         verdict nsim #> 1 condition  0.05 0.075 0.01574218 0.2038647 well-calibrated   40

title: Validation: is your power number trustworthy?
text: mp_plan() one-call version recommended workflow: calibrates null, suggests inference method, estimates power, reports three together. design anti-conservative, says prominently – power number trustworthy. mp_power() also nudge toward check run directly risky design (clusters complex random effects) without calibration record; mp_attach_calibration() records check done.
code: mp_plan(scn, nsim = 40, calibrate_nsim = 40, seed = 1) #> <mp_plan> #>   alpha: 0.05 #>   1. calibration (null): Type I = 0.0750 (95% CI 0.0157, 0.2039) -> well-calibrated #>   2. method: wald (consider kenward-roger, satterthwaite, pb) #>   3. power: 65.0% (95% CI 48.3%, 79.4%)

what: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: mixpower provides simulation-based power sample-size analysis linear generalized linear mixed-effects models (Gaussian, binomial, Poisson, negative-binomial families) fitted lme4. pairs backend-agnostic simulation engine publication-ready diagnostics: exact (Clopper-Pearson) power intervals, Monte Carlo standard errors, Type S/M error rates, convergence/singular-fit reporting.

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: Install released version CRAN: development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("mixpower") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("alitovchenko/mixpower")

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: power number trustworthy test controls Type error. recommended workflow calibrate null, choose inference method, estimate power — mp_plan() three reports together: risky designs (clusters complex random effects), mp_power() nudges toward mp_calibrate() skip step; attach calibration mp_attach_calibration() record check done, pass check_calibration = FALSE silence.
code: d <- mp_design(list(subject = 12), trials_per_cell = 8) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.4),   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5)),   residual_sd = 1 ) scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ condition + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a)  mp_plan(scn, nsim = 1000, seed = 1) #> 1. calibration (null): Type I = ... -> well-calibrated / anti-conservative #> 2. method: ... #> 3. power:  ...% (95% CI ...)

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: mp_power_adaptive() simulates batches stops soon estimate precise enough decision clear, reporting many replicates actually needed:
code: # stop when the 95% CI half-width <= 0.02 (or at 5000 sims): mp_power_adaptive(scn, stop = mp_stop(ci_halfwidth = 0.02), seed = 1)  # or stop as soon as the CI clearly clears a target power of 0.8: mp_power_adaptive(scn, stop = mp_stop(target = 0.8, ci_halfwidth = 0.03), seed = 1)

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: mp_report() turns result Markdown trust report — design, assumptions, calibration, power interval Monte Carlo precision, diagnostics, reproducibility manifest, explicit caveats fragile claims — ready drop methods section, grant, registered report:
code: report <- mp_report(mp_plan(scn, nsim = 1000, seed = 1)) report                                   # prints the Markdown mp_write_report(report, "power-report.md")

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: Initial release. Supports simulation-based power analysis Gaussian linear mixed-effects models. Includes diagnostics convergence simulation uncertainty.

what: Power from a fitted model (pilot data)
title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: already fitted lmer/glmer model, mp_from_fit() turns scenario directly — like simr, mixpower’s diagnostics (Type S/M, exact CIs), df-corrected tests, effect-size sensitivity.
code: library(lme4) pilot <- lmer(Reaction ~ Days + (Days | Subject), data = sleepstudy)  scn <- mp_from_fit(pilot, test_term = "Days") mp_power(scn, nsim = 200, seed = 1)            # data-based power  # Smallest-effect-of-interest: vary the effect while keeping the fitted # variance components. mp_sensitivity(scn, vary = list(`fixed_effects.Days` = c(2, 5, 10)),                nsim = 200, seed = 1)  # Scale the pilot's sample size up or down (simr-style extend) and curve power: mp_power(mp_extend(scn, Subject = 60), nsim = 200, seed = 1) mp_power_curve(scn, vary = list(`extend.Subject` = c(20, 40, 60, 80)),                nsim = 200, seed = 1)

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: Specify effects way reason — ICC, standardized effect baseline probability odds ratio — mp_elicit() solves coherent set assumptions prints implied model:
code: # Gaussian: medium effect, ICC = 0.1 a <- mp_elicit("gaussian", d = 0.5, icc = 0.1)  # Binomial: 20% baseline rate, odds ratio 1.8, subject ICC 0.05 a <- mp_elicit("binomial", baseline_prob = 0.2, odds_ratio = 1.8, icc = 0.05)  scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ condition + (1 | subject),                         design = mp_design(list(subject = 30), trials_per_cell = 8),                         assumptions = a)

what: Smallest effect of interest & safeguard power
title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: Plan power around smallest effect size interest instead optimistic pilot estimate. mp_sesoi() shrinks focal effect (default 15%), mp_safeguard_effect() derives conservative, uncertainty-aware effect fit’s confidence bound (Perugini et al., 2014).
code: # 15% reduction (a conservative SESOI heuristic) mp_power(mp_sesoi(scn, multiplier = 0.85), nsim = 200, seed = 1)  # Safeguard: use the CI bound nearest zero from the pilot fit sg <- mp_safeguard_effect(pilot, term = "Days", conf_level = 0.90) mp_power(mp_sesoi(scn, effect = sg), nsim = 200, seed = 1)

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: random_effects supports one random slopes per grouping factor scalar full-matrix correlation. fixed effect name becomes balanced design predictor, several factors crossed automatically.
code: a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, x1 = 0.5, x2 = 0.3),   random_effects = list(subject = list(     intercept_sd = 0.4,     slopes = list(x1 = 0.3, x2 = 0.3),     cor = 0.1   )),   residual_sd = 1 ) scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ x1 + x2 + (1 + x1 + x2 | subject),                         design = mp_design(list(subject = 30), trials_per_cell = 8),                         assumptions = a, predictor = "x1") mp_power(scn, nsim = 200, seed = 1)

what: Realistic designs: continuous covariates, nesting, imbalance
title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: mp_design() goes beyond single balanced binary factor: declare continuous -subject predictors, nest subjects higher grouping factor (three levels), set unbalanced within-subject sample sizes. anything built-grammar doesn’t cover natively — partially nested designs, multi-arm factors, nonlinear time, correlated predictors — supply custom data-generating function; ’s validated runs full power / calibration / comparison / reporting pipeline:
code: # Continuous within-subject (time-like) predictor: mp_design(list(subject = 30), trials_per_cell = 6,           predictors = list(time = "continuous"))  # Three-level: 8 sites, 5 subjects per site, 4 trials each: mp_design(list(site = 8, subject = 5), trials_per_cell = 4,           nesting = c(subject = "site"))  # Unbalanced within-subject sizes (recycled across subjects): mp_design(list(subject = 20), trials_per_cell = c(3, 5, 8))  # Cluster-randomized trial with 2:1 allocation (treatment assigned per site): mp_design(list(site = 20, subject = 10), trials_per_cell = 1,           nesting = c(subject = "site"),           predictors = list(arm = list(level = "cluster", allocation = 1/3)))  # Longitudinal AR(1) residual autocorrelation (on the assumptions): mp_assumptions(list(`(Intercept)` = 0, time = 0.3),                random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5)),                residual_sd = 1, residual_ar1 = 0.5) my_dgp <- function(scenario, seed = NULL) {   # ... return a data.frame with the formula's variables ... } mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ arm + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a,                  simulate = my_dgp, test_method = "lrt",                  null_formula = y ~ 1 + (1 | subject))

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: Reflect realistic incomplete data mp_missing(): missing-completely--random, missing--random (probability depends observed column), monotone longitudinal dropout (per-timepoint proportions Weibull dropout time).
code: # 20% MCAR deletion: mp_power(mp_missing(scn, "mcar", prob = 0.2), nsim = 200, seed = 1)  # Monotone dropout along a time predictor: mp_power(mp_missing(scn, "dropout", time = "time",                     dropout = c(0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.35, 0.5, 0.6)), nsim = 200, seed = 1)

what: Flexible analysis: omnibus tests, contrasts, model comparison
title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
text: Test several coefficients jointly (omnibus), custom linear contrast, compare competing analysis models simulated data expose Type inflation misspecification.
code: # Omnibus joint Wald test of two terms: mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ x1 + x2 + (1 | subject), d, a, test_term = c("x1", "x2"))  # Custom linear contrast (e.g. emmeans-style weights): mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ condition + (1 | subject), d, a, contrast = c(condition = 1))  # Same-data comparison: reports each model's power AND Type I error, and # recommends the best-powered plan that still controls Type I: mp_compare_models(list(maximal = scn_max, reduced = scn_int), nsim = 500, seed = 1)

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 30), trials_per_cell = 4) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.3),   residual_sd = 1 ) scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a )  sens <- mp_sensitivity(   scn,   vary = list(`fixed_effects.condition` = c(0.2, 0.4, 0.6)),   nsim = 50,   seed = 123 ) plot(sens)

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 30), trials_per_cell = 4) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.3),   residual_sd = 1 ) scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a )  curve <- mp_power_curve(   scn,   vary = list(`clusters.subject` = c(20, 30, 40, 50)),   nsim = 50,   seed = 123 ) plot(curve)  solve <- mp_solve_sample_size(   scn,   parameter = "clusters.subject",   grid = c(20, 30, 40, 50),   target_power = 0.8,   nsim = 50,   seed = 123 ) solve$solution

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 40), trials_per_cell = 8) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.4),   residual_sd = 1,   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.1)) )  scn_wald <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d, assumptions = a,   test_method = "wald" )  scn_lrt <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d, assumptions = a,   test_method = "lrt",   null_formula = y ~ 1 + (1 | subject) )  vary_spec <- list(`clusters.subject` = c(30, 50, 80)) sens_wald <- mp_sensitivity(scn_wald, vary = vary_spec, nsim = 50, seed = 123) sens_lrt  <- mp_sensitivity(scn_lrt,  vary = vary_spec, nsim = 50, seed = 123)

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models
code: d <- mp_design(clusters = list(subject = 40), trials_per_cell = 8) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list(`(Intercept)` = 0, condition = 0.4),   residual_sd = 1,   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.1)) )  scn_wald <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a,   predictor = "condition",   subject = "subject",   outcome = "y",   test_method = "wald" )  scn_lrt <- mp_scenario_lme4(   y ~ condition + (1 | subject),   design = d,   assumptions = a,   predictor = "condition",   subject = "subject",   outcome = "y",   test_method = "lrt",   null_formula = y ~ 1 + (1 | subject) )  vary_spec <- list(`clusters.subject` = c(30, 50, 80))  sens_wald <- mp_sensitivity(scn_wald, vary = vary_spec, nsim = 50, seed = 123) sens_lrt  <- mp_sensitivity(scn_lrt,  vary = vary_spec, nsim = 50, seed = 123)  comp <- rbind(   transform(sens_wald$results, method = "wald"),   transform(sens_lrt$results,  method = "lrt") )  comp  wald_dat <- comp[comp$method == "wald", ] lrt_dat  <- comp[comp$method == "lrt", ]  x <- "clusters.subject"  plot(   wald_dat[[x]], wald_dat$estimate,   type = "b", pch = 16, lty = 1,   ylim = c(0, 1),   xlab = x, ylab = "Power estimate",   col = "steelblue" ) lines(   lrt_dat[[x]], lrt_dat$estimate,   type = "b", pch = 17, lty = 2,   col = "firebrick" ) legend(   "bottomright",   legend = c("Wald", "LRT"),   col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),   lty = c(1, 2), pch = c(16, 17), bty = "n" )  diag_comp <- comp[, c(   "method",   "clusters.subject",   "estimate", "mcse", "conf_low", "conf_high",   "failure_rate", "singular_rate", "n_effective", "nsim" )]  diag_comp[order(diag_comp$method, diag_comp$`clusters.subject`), ]

what: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models — mixpower-package
title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models — mixpower-package
text: mixpower simulation-based toolkit power sample-size analysis linear generalized linear mixed-effects models (LMMs GLMMs). design-first (pilot data required), supports Gaussian, binomial, Poisson, negative binomial families via lme4; Wald likelihood-ratio tests; multi-parameter sensitivity grids; power curves minimum sample-size solvers; parallel evaluation deterministic seeds; full reproducibility (manifests, result bundling, export CSV/JSON). Every run reports diagnostics (failure rate, singular-fit rate, effective N).

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models — mixpower-package
text: Typical workflow: (1) define design mp_design() (cluster sizes, trials per cell) effect-size assumptions mp_assumptions(); (2) build scenario backend constructor (e.g. mp_scenario_lme4() Gaussian LMM); (3) run mp_power() single power estimate, mp_sensitivity() vary parameters, mp_power_curve() / mp_solve_sample_size() curves sample-size. Use fixed seed reproducibility. Failure singular-fit rates always reported never suppressed.

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models — mixpower-package
text: loading package, define design assumptions, build lme4 scenario, call mp_power(). See Examples minimal runnable workflow.

title: Simulation-Based Power Analysis for Mixed-Effects Models — mixpower-package
text: Design assumptions: mp_design(), mp_assumptions(). Scenarios (LMM/GLMM): mp_scenario(), mp_scenario_lme4(), mp_scenario_lme4_binomial(), mp_scenario_lme4_poisson(), mp_scenario_lme4_nb(). Power sensitivity: mp_power() (optional aggregate = "streaming"), mp_sensitivity(), mp_sensitivity_parallel(), mp_power_curve(), mp_power_curve_parallel(), mp_solve_sample_size(), mp_grid_sample_size(), mp_quick_power(). Backends simulators: mp_backend(), validate_mp_backend(), mp_backend_lme4(), mp_backend_lme4_binomial(), mp_backend_lme4_poisson(), mp_backend_lme4_nb(), mp_backend_glmmtmb(), mp_scenario_glmmtmb_lmm(), simulate_glmm_binomial_data(), simulate_glmm_poisson_data(), simulate_glmm_nb_data(). Optional tidyverse (Suggests): tibble::as_tibble() ggplot2::autoplot() methods. Reproducibility reporting: mp_manifest(), mp_bundle_results(), mp_report_table(), mp_write_results(). Plotting: plot() mp_power_curve mp_sensitivity objects.

title: Create modeling assumptions for simulation-based power — mp_assumptions
text: fixed_effects Named list numeric values (e.g., list("(Intercept)" = 0, condition = 0.4)). random_effects Optional named list keyed grouping factor. element named list intercept_sd (random-intercept SD linear-predictor scale) , optionally, slopes (named list random-slope SDs, one per predictor) cor. example, list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5, slopes = list(condition = 0.3), cor = 0.2)) encodes correlated random intercept slope, .e. (1 + condition | subject). several slopes, cor may single scalar (applied every pair terms) full correlation matrix c("(Intercept)", names(slopes)); fixed effect named fixed_effects also becomes balanced design predictor. icc Deprecated. Previously documented intraclass correlation used random-intercept SD. supplied interpreted intercept_sd folded random_effects warning. Use random_effects instead. residual_sd Optional non-negative numeric residual SD (Gaussian). residual_ar1 Optional within-subject lag-1 autocorrelation Gaussian residuals, (-1, 1), longitudinal designs (residuals drawn stationary AR(1) process ordered within subject). Ignored non-Gaussian families. notes Optional free text.

Defaults scenario's test term (first non-intercept fixed effect). nsim Number null simulations (default 1000; Type estimation needs replicates power point estimate tight interval). alpha Nominal significance level checked (default 0.05). seed Optional seed reproducibility. conf_level Confidence level Type interval (default 0.95). failure_policy Passed mp_power().

title: Check the Type I error calibration of a scenario's test — mp_calibrate
text: "well-calibrated": interval contains alpha. "anti-conservative": interval lies entirely alpha (test rejects often — e.g. Wald test clusters, model omits random slope actually present data-generating process). Power computed test trustworthy. title: Plug a custom data-generating function into the mixpower pipeline — mp_custom_dgp
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   # A three-arm design (omnibus test) via a custom DGP.   dgp <- function(scenario, seed = NULL) {     n <- scenario$design$clusters$subject     b <- scenario$assumptions$fixed_effects     arm <- factor(rep(c("a", "b", "c"), length.out = n))     mu <- c(a = 0, b = b$arm_b, c = b$arm_c)[as.character(arm)]     data.frame(subject = factor(seq_len(n)), arm = arm,                y = mu + stats::rnorm(n, sd = scenario$assumptions$residual_sd))   }   d <- mp_design(list(subject = 90), trials_per_cell = 1)   a <- mp_assumptions(list("(Intercept)" = 0, arm_b = 0.4, arm_c = 0.7),                       residual_sd = 1)   scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ arm + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a,                           simulate = dgp, test_method = "lrt",                           null_formula = y ~ 1 + (1 | subject))   mp_power(scn, nsim = 20, seed = 1) } #> <mp_power> #>   nsim: 20 #>   alpha: 0.05 #>   failure_policy: count_as_nondetect #>   power: 0.000 #>   mcse: 0.000 #>   95% CI (clopper-pearson): [0.000, 0.168] #>   diagnostics: #>     - fail_rate: 1.000 #>     - singular_rate: 0.000 #>     - type_s: NA #>     - type_m: NA # }

title: Elicit assumptions from standardized scientific quantities — mp_elicit
text: family "gaussian" "binomial". predictor Name focal predictor (default "condition"). d Standardized effect (Cohen's d) "gaussian". odds_ratio, risk_ratio Effect "binomial" (give one). baseline_prob Control-group event probability "binomial". outcome_sd Total outcome SD "gaussian" (default 1). icc Intraclass correlation: single value (applied groups) vector named group. "gaussian" values must sum < 1. slope Random-slope SD fraction random-intercept SD: single value named group (NULL none). cor Intercept-slope correlation: single value named group. groups Grouping-factor name(s) (default "subject"). intercept Fixed intercept; defaults 0 (gaussian) qlogis(baseline_prob) (binomial).

title: Elicit assumptions from standardized scientific quantities — mp_elicit

Predictor correlations, autocorrelation, cluster-size distributions design choices (see mp_design()); missingness mp_missing().

text: scenario mp_scenario. nsim Positive integer number simulations. alpha Significance threshold detection (default 0.05). seed Optional seed reproducibility. failure_policy treat failed fits / missing p-values: "count_as_nondetect" (default): failures count non-detections. "exclude": drop failures denominator (always reported). keep store: "minimal": per-sim summary rows. "fits": also store fit objects (may large). "data": also store simulated data (can large). conf_level Confidence level power interval (default 0.95). ci_method Power CI type: "clopper-pearson" (default, exact binomial) "wald" (normal approximation). aggregate "full" (default) stores every replicate sims. "streaming" accumulates counts (lower memory); requires keep = "minimal" returns empty sims data frame column names full run. check_calibration TRUE (default), warn per session design risky Type error (clusters /complex random effects) calibration record, nudging toward mp_calibrate() / mp_plan(). advice always available $calibration_advice result. Set FALSE silence (used internally parameter sweeps).

title: Simulation-based power estimation (engine-agnostic core) — mp_power

title: Simulation-based power estimation (engine-agnostic core) — mp_power
text: Phase 4 core, scenario must provide engine functions: simulate_fun, fit_fun, test_fun. Later phases supply defaults based specific backends (e.g., lme4). Diagnostics include Type S (wrong-sign) Type M (exaggeration-ratio) errors among significant replicates (Gelman & Carlin, 2014), computed tested term's true effect known non-zero backend reports estimate.

title: Simulation-based power estimation (engine-agnostic core) — mp_power
code: # A tiny toy engine (not mixed models) just to demonstrate the workflow: d <- mp_design(list(subject = 30), trials_per_cell = 1) a <- mp_assumptions(list(condition = 0.3), residual_sd = 1)  sim_fun <- function(scn, seed) {   n <- scn$design$clusters$subject   x <- stats::rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)   y <- scn$assumptions$fixed_effects$condition * x +     stats::rnorm(n, sd = scn$assumptions$residual_sd)   data.frame(y = y, condition = x) } fit_fun <- function(dat, scn) stats::lm(scn$formula, data = dat) test_fun <- function(fit, scn) {   sm <- summary(fit)   p <- sm$coefficients["condition", "Pr(>|t|)"]   list(p_value = as.numeric(p)) }  s <- mp_scenario(   y ~ condition, d, a,   simulate_fun = sim_fun,   fit_fun = fit_fun,   test_fun = test_fun ) res <- mp_power(s, nsim = 50, seed = 1) summary(res) #> $power #> [1] 0.06 #>  #> $mcse #> [1] 0.03358571 #>  #> $ci #> [1] 0.01254859 0.16548195 #>  #> $ci_method #> [1] "clopper-pearson" #>  #> $diagnostics #> $diagnostics$fail_rate #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$singular_rate #> [1] 0 #>  #> $diagnostics$type_s #> [1] NA #>  #> $diagnostics$type_m #> [1] NA #>  #>  #> $nsim #> [1] 50 #>  #> $alpha #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $failure_policy #> [1] "count_as_nondetect" #>  #> $conf_level #> [1] 0.95 #>

what: Recommend an inference method for a scenario — mp_recommend_method
title: Recommend an inference method for a scenario — mp_recommend_method
text: Heuristic guidance test_method scenario, based number levels random grouping factors. Wald (normal-approximation z/t) tests , lesser extent, likelihood-ratio tests known anti-conservative number clusters small (Luke, 2017): degrees freedom overstated, test rejects often. clusters, degrees--freedom-corrected test (Satterthwaite Kenward-Roger, linear mixed models) parametric bootstrap (family) controls Type error far better.

title: Recommend an inference method for a scenario — mp_recommend_method
code: d <- mp_design(list(subject = 12), trials_per_cell = 8) a <- mp_assumptions(   fixed_effects = list("(Intercept)" = 0, condition = 0.4),   random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5)),   residual_sd = 1 ) scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ condition + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a) mp_recommend_method(scn) #> <mp_recommendation> #>   current method: wald #>   smallest grouping factor: 12 levels #>   caution: yes #>   recommended: kenward-roger, satterthwaite, pb #>   Smallest grouping factor has 12 levels (< 30). 'wald' tests can be anti-conservative with few clusters; prefer 'kenward-roger' or 'satterthwaite' or 'pb'. Verify with mp_calibrate().

code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {   d <- mp_design(list(subject = 12), trials_per_cell = 8)   a <- mp_assumptions(list("(Intercept)" = 0, condition = 0.4),                       random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5)),                       residual_sd = 1)   scn <- mp_scenario_lme4(y ~ condition + (1 | subject), design = d, assumptions = a)   mp_report(mp_plan(scn, nsim = 100, seed = 1)) } #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular') #> # Power analysis report #>  #> _Generated 2026-06-27 18:20 UTC with mixpower 1.2.0._ #>  #> ## Summary #>  #> A simulation-based power analysis was conducted using the mixpower R package (version 1.2.0). Data were simulated under the model y ~ condition + (1 | subject), with 12 subject (8 observations each). The focal effect (condition) was set to 0.4 and tested with a Wald z/t test at alpha = 0.05. Across 100 simulated datasets, estimated power was 45.0% (95% Clopper-Pearson confidence interval 35.0% to 55.3%). Among significant replicates the average exaggeration ratio (Type M) was 1.48. #>  #> ## Design and model #>  #> - model: `y ~ condition + (1 | subject)` (Gaussian linear mixed model) #> - clusters: 12 subject; observations: 8 per subject #> - test: wald on `condition` #>  #> ## Data-generating assumptions #>  #> - fixed effects: (Intercept) = 0, condition = 0.4 #> - random effects (SD on the linear-predictor scale): #>     - subject: intercept_sd = 0.5 #> - residual SD: 1 #>  #> ## Type I calibration #>  #> - empirical Type I at alpha = 0.05: **0.0800** (95% CI 0.0352, 0.1516) -> **well-calibrated** #>  #> - inference-method guidance: current `wald` (consider kenward-roger, satterthwaite, pb) #>  #> ## Power #>  #> - estimate: **45.0%** (95% clopper-pearson CI 35.0%, 55.3%) #> - Monte Carlo SE: 0.0497; replicates: 100 #>  #> ## Diagnostics #>  #> - model-fit failure rate: 0.0% #> - singular-fit rate: 3.0% #> - Type S (wrong sign): 0.0% #> - Type M (exaggeration ratio): 1.48 #>  #> ## Reproducibility #>  #> - seed: 1 (fixed) #> - R 4.6.1, mixpower 1.2.0 #> - scenario digest: 54b3e4b77e73b5ea #> - git: afdfbd3fee #>  #> ## Caveats and fragile claims #>  #> - The chosen inference method may be anti-conservative for this cluster count; consider kenward-roger, satterthwaite, pb. #> - Statistically significant estimates exaggerate the effect by ~1.5x (Type M); the design is underpowered for unbiased estimation. #> - The power estimate is imprecise (wide confidence interval); increase nsim or use mp_power_adaptive(). #>  #>  # }

title: mixpower 1.2.0
text: mp_plan() runs recommended end--end workflow — calibrate null, recommend inference method, estimate power — reports together, power estimate never shown without Type error check says whether trustworthy. mp_power() now nudges (per session) toward mp_calibrate() / mp_plan() run risky design (clusters complex random effects) calibration record. advice always available $calibration_advice; pass check_calibration = FALSE silence (internal parameter sweeps already ). mp_attach_calibration() records calibration result scenario. mp_report_table() gains mp_plan method. DESCRIPTION now lists CRAN URL.

title: mixpower 1.2.0
text: mp_power_adaptive() runs mp_power() batches stops soon power interval precise enough (ci_halfwidth) accept/reject decision vs target unambiguous, instead relying arbitrary fixed nsim. reports achieved Monte Carlo precision number replicates actually used, result identical fixed mp_power() run nsim. mp_stop() specifies stopping rule (ci_halfwidth, target, min_nsim, max_nsim, batch).

title: mixpower 1.2.0
text: mp_report() assembles reviewer-ready Markdown report power (mp_plan()) result: design, model, data-generating assumptions, Type calibration, inference-method guidance, power interval Monte Carlo precision, diagnostics (failure/singular rates, Type S/M), missing-data assumptions, reproducibility manifest, explicit warnings fragile unsupported claims. mp_write_report() writes Markdown (HTML via rmarkdown/pandoc).

title: mixpower 1.2.0
text: mp_compare_models() now reports candidate analysis model’s Type error (estimated null simulated data) alongside power, calibration verdict per model recommended plan (best-powered model still controls Type error). answers planning question “analysis valid well powered?” — useful preregistration grant methods sections. Set calibrate = FALSE power .

title: mixpower 1.2.0
text: mp_elicit() builds coherent mp_assumptions standardized quantities researchers actually reason — per-group ICC, standardized effect (Cohen’s d) baseline probability odds/risk ratio, random-slope variability — prints implied model. solves Gaussian variance partition binomial latent-scale mapping internally, longer hand-pick raw coefficients SDs.

title: mixpower 1.2.0
text: mp_design(predictors = ...) gains cluster-level treatment assignment (level = "cluster") cluster-randomized designs — predictor constant within nesting cluster — unequal allocation ratios binary /cluster predictors. mp_assumptions(residual_ar1 = ...) adds AR(1) within-subject residual autocorrelation longitudinal Gaussian models. design print() now shows predictor’s type/level/allocation.

title: mixpower 1.2.0
text: mp_custom_dgp() wraps user-supplied data-generating function validated simulate_fun, mp_scenario_lme4(simulate = ...) plugs keeping lme4 fit/test. Designs built-grammar cover natively — partially nested designs, multi-arm factors, nonlinear time trends, correlated predictors — now run full power / calibration / comparison / reporting pipeline. output validated contain model formula’s variables.

title: mixpower 1.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-25 Reduce overall R CMD check time well CRAN’s budget: vignettes now use small toy designs iterations, heavy simulation/correctness tests marked skip_on_cran() (still run continuous integration lme4 path exercised examples). user-facing changes.

title: mixpower 1.1.0
text: large feature release focused credibility design realism.

title: mixpower 1.1.0
text: mp_calibrate() estimates empirical Type error rate null returns verdict (well-calibrated / anti-conservative / conservative), flagging analyses (e.g. omitted random slope, Wald clusters) whose power trustworthy. mp_recommend_method() gives design-based inference-method guidance.

title: mixpower 1.1.0
text: mp_design() gains continuous -subject predictors (predictors=), three-level nested grouping factors (nesting=), unbalanced within-subject sample sizes (vector trials_per_cell). mp_missing() adds missing-data / dropout mechanisms: MCAR, MAR (logit observed column), monotone longitudinal dropout (per-timepoint proportions Weibull dropout time).

title: mixpower 1.1.0
text: mp_extend() extend.<group> sensitivity key scale mp_from_fit() scenario’s sample size (simr extend() analogue). Effect-size converters (mp_d_to_beta(), mp_r2_to_beta(), mp_icc_to_sd(), mp_or_to_logodds(), mp_t_to_beta(), mp_f_to_beta(), inverses) translate standardized/published effects model coefficients.

title: mixpower 1.1.0
text: Omnibus joint-Wald tests several coefficients (test_term = c(...)), custom linear contrasts (contrast=), mp_compare_models(), fits multiple analysis models simulated data expose Type inflation misspecification.

title: mixpower 1.1.0
text: mp_power_checkpoint() runs power simulations resumable, persisted batches. mp_methods_text() generates methods-section paragraph; plot() mp_power object shows p-value distribution; mp_report_table() accepts calibration results.

title: mixpower 1.0.0
text: First stable release initial CRAN submission. Consolidates 0.x series documented, tested baseline: simulation-based power sample-size analysis Gaussian, binomial, Poisson, negative-binomial mixed models via ‘lme4’; Wald / LRT / Satterthwaite / Kenward-Roger / parametric-bootstrap tests; multiple correlated random slopes; mp_from_fit(); sensitivity grids, power curves, sample-size solvers; SESOI / safeguard helpers; exact power intervals Type S/M diagnostics; parallel evaluation reproducible result bundling.

title: mixpower 0.7.0
text: Multiple correlated random slopes. random_effects now accepts several slopes per grouping factor, correlation cor given either scalar (applied every pair terms) full correlation matrix c("(Intercept)", names(slopes)). fixed effect named fixed_effects becomes balanced design predictor, multi-factor maximal models (1 + x1 + x2 | subject) simulate correctly. simulator draws random-effect block single multivariate normal (Cholesky / eigen), new dependencies; single-slope behaviour unchanged. mp_sesoi() returns scenario whose focal effect replaced smallest effect size interest — either explicit value scaled version assumed effect (default multiplier = 0.85, 15% reduction). mp_safeguard_effect() derives conservative, uncertainty-aware effect fitted model: confidence-interval bound nearest zero (Perugini, Gallucci & Costantini, 2014). Pipe mp_sesoi(effect = ...) safeguard-power analysis. mp_from_fit() now records full random-effect structure (every slope complete correlation matrix) scenario assumptions. mp_sensitivity() / mp_power_curve() can vary random_effects.<g>.cor random_effects.<g>.slopes.<predictor> addition intercept_sd. New vignette, Effect sizes: maximal models, fitted pilots, safeguard power, tying features together Type S / Type M interpretation.

title: mixpower 0.6.0
text: mp_from_fit() builds power scenario existing lmer/glmer fit (pilot published model), workflow simr known . New responses simulated fitted model via stats::simulate() (preserving estimated random-effect structure residual variance), refit, tested. fixed effects come scenario assumptions (starting fitted coefficients), mp_sensitivity() / mp_power_curve() can vary effect size data-based vs smallest-effect--interest comparisons. mixpower’s inference methods, exact CIs, Type S/M diagnostics apply.

title: mixpower 0.5.0
text: Exact Clopper-Pearson confidence intervals power estimate now default (mp_power(ci_method = "clopper-pearson")); "wald" remains available. previous Wald interval mis-behaved near power 0 1 (e.g. returning [0, 0]). Type S (sign) Type M (magnitude/exaggeration) error rates (Gelman & Carlin, 2014) reported mp_power() diagnostics mp_report_table(), computed among significant replicates tested term’s true effect known non-zero. Type M > 1 flags statistically significant estimates exaggerate effect (acute low power). Backends now also return fixed-effect estimate per replicate (used Type S/M); custom engines can supply estimate test_fun output.

title: mixpower 0.4.0
text: Fixed defect likelihood-ratio tests (test_method = "lrt") returned NA every replicate (hence power 0) simulation loop: null model refit update(), resolve simulated data scope. models now refit explicitly fitted model’s data frame. affected lme4 (Gaussian GLMM) glmmTMB backends.

title: mixpower 0.4.0
text: New small-sample inference methods Gaussian lme4 backend: test_method = "satterthwaite" "kenward-roger" (df-corrected t tests via lmerTest/pbkrtest), recommended Wald-z number groups modest. Kenward-Roger refit REML internally requires. New test_method = "pb" (parametric-bootstrap LRT via pbkrtest) lme4 LMM GLMM backends, pb_nsim control (default 100). exact-er small samples costly: power replicate refits model pb_nsim times. Inference now centralized one dispatcher shared backends, test logic defined rather duplicated per family.

title: mixpower 0.3.0
text: Random slopes. random_effects now accepts random slope intercept-slope correlation per grouping factor, e.g. random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5,   slopes = list(condition = 0.3), cor = 0.2)), simulating (1 + condition |   subject). essential correctly-sized power: omitting present random slope inflates Type error (Barr et al., 2013).

title: mixpower 0.3.0
text: Unified data-generating engine. Gaussian, binomial, Poisson, negative-binomial simulators now share single formula-agnostic core (.mp_simulate_mixed) builds balanced within-subject design, adds fixed effects correlated random effects (intercept + optional slope, via base-R Cholesky draw new dependency), applies family response. four simulate_* functions thin wrappers, random-effect handling slopes defined rather duplicated per family.

title: mixpower 0.2.0
text: Fixed severe defect every lme4-family Wald test errored internally (base::diag() S4 dpoMatrix vcov(merMod)), mp_power() silently returned 0 lme4 Gaussian/binomial/Poisson/NB designs. standard error now read fitted-model coefficient table. Power correct (≈1 strongly-powered design; ≈alpha null). Fixed defect Gaussian backends ignored random-intercept SD: mp_scenario_lme4() / mp_scenario_glmmtmb_lmm() always simulated subject SD 1, mp_sensitivity() / mp_power_curve() random-effect size effect. Random-effect SDs now flow mp_assumptions() every family (Gaussian, binomial, Poisson, negative binomial).

title: mixpower 0.2.0
text: mp_assumptions() gains random_effects, correctly-named specification random-effect sizes standard deviations linear-predictor scale, e.g. random_effects = list(subject = list(intercept_sd = 0.5)). icc deprecated. documented intraclass correlation used random-intercept SD; now interpreted intercept_sd, folded random_effects, warns per session. previous [0, 1) constraint (wrongly blocked realistic SDs > 1) removed. mp_sensitivity() / mp_power_curve() can vary random_effects.<group>.intercept_sd.

title: mixpower 0.2.0
text: New glmmTMB backend (mp_backend_glmmtmb(), mp_scenario_glmmtmb_lmm()). Formalised backend contract (mp_backend(), validate_mp_backend()). Parallel sensitivity power curves; streaming mp_power(). Sample-size solver (mp_solve_sample_size()) power curves (mp_power_curve()). Reproducibility layer: manifests, result bundling, CSV/JSON export. Tidy S3 integration (as_tibble(), autoplot()) mp_quick_power().

title: mixpower 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-12 Initial release. Supports simulation-based power analysis Gaussian linear mixed-effects models. Includes diagnostics convergence simulation uncertainty.
